title: Improve semantic dedup error handling with recover() and detailed logging
body:
## Background

From PR #314 code review finding: When semantic deduplication fails, the code logs a warning and treats all findings as unique. However:
1. No panic recovery - if the comparer panics, the entire review process crashes
2. Limited error context for debugging in production

## Current Behavior

```go
if err != nil {
    log.Printf("warning: semantic dedup failed: %v (treating all as unique)", err)
    return findings, 0, nil
}
```

## Suggested Improvements

1. Wrap `CompareAgainstExisting` in a `recover()` block
2. Include candidate count and error details in warning logs
3. Consider returning a wrapped error so callers can decide whether to fail fast

## Affected Files

- internal/usecase/github/poster.go (around line 984)

## Labels

enhancement, error-handling
